root_cause.text: The application insecurely deserializes untrusted data by using the default `ObjectInputStream` in `TcpSocketServer` and `UdpSocketServer` without restricting which Java classes can be instantiated.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces `new ObjectInputStream(inputStream)` in `ObjectInputStreamLogEventBridge` with `new FilteredObjectInputStream(inputStream, allowedClasses)`, which overrides `resolveClass` to strictly whitelist allowable classes.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The application insecurely deserializes untrusted data by using the default `ObjectInputStream` in `TcpSocketServer` and `UdpSocketServer` without restricting which Java classes can be instantiated.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces `new ObjectInputStream(inputStream)` in `ObjectInputStreamLogEventBridge` with `new FilteredObjectInputStream(inputStream, allowedClasses)`, which overrides `resolveClass` to strictly whitelist allowable classes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add imports for Collections and List utility classes.
intent_record.what: Added import statements for java.util.Collections and java.util.List.
intent_record.how: Makes Collections and List available for use in the file without fully qualified names.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add command line parameter to configure allowed classes for deserialization.
intent_record.what: Added a new command line parameter field for allowed classes.
intent_record.how: Allows users to specify additional classes that are permitted during deserialization.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add allowedClasses configuration methods to support class filtering.
intent_record.what: Added getter and setter methods for an allowedClasses property.
intent_record.how: Provides a way to store and retrieve a list of allowed class names, returning an empty list if none are set.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add support for class filtering during deserialization to improve security.
intent_record.what: Added a new constructor to accept a list of allowed classes and imported FilteredObjectInputStream.
intent_record.how: Provides a mechanism to restrict the classes that can be deserialized by storing an allowed list of class names.

intent_record.intent_summary: Secure object deserialization by using a FilteredObjectInputStream to enforce an allowed classes list.
intent_record.what: Replaced the instantiation of ObjectInputStream with FilteredObjectInputStream.
intent_record.how: Restricts object deserialization to a predefined list of allowed classes, preventing the instantiation of arbitrary or potentially malicious objects.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add imports for Collections and List to support additional data structure usage in the file.
intent_record.what: Added import statements for java.util.Collections and java.util.List.
intent_record.how: Resolves compilation dependencies for Collections and List classes within the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add support for specifying allowed classes during deserialization to improve security.
intent_record.what: Added a new overloaded method to accept a list of allowed classes for deserialization and updated the existing method to delegate to it.
intent_record.how: Provides a mechanism to restrict which classes can be deserialized, enhancing security against arbitrary code execution.

intent_record.intent_summary: Pass allowed classes to the serialized socket server to restrict deserialization.
intent_record.what: Added the cla.getAllowedClasses() argument to the createSerializedSocketServer method call.
intent_record.how: Passes the list of allowed classes to the socket server, enabling class filtering during object deserialization.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add import statement for java.util.List to support subsequent code changes.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for java.util.List.
intent_record.how: Makes the java.util.List interface available for use within the class, resolving compilation errors for List usage.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a factory method to restrict deserialization to a specific list of allowed classes.
intent_record.what: Adds a new factory method createSerializedSocketServer that accepts a list of allowed classes.
intent_record.how: Provides a way to specify acceptable classes during deserialization, which limits potential execution of arbitrary code.

intent_record.intent_summary: Pass allowed classes to the serialized socket server to restrict object deserialization.
intent_record.what: Added the allowed classes argument to the createSerializedSocketServer method call.
intent_record.how: Passes a list of permitted classes to the server creation logic, which enables filtering during object deserialization.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add FilteredObjectInputStream to restrict object deserialization to explicitly allowed classes.
intent_record.what: Added a new FilteredObjectInputStream class that extends ObjectInputStream.
intent_record.how: Overrides resolveClass to enforce an allowlist of classes during deserialization, throwing an InvalidObjectException for unauthorized classes.

grouping_result.intent: Add imports for Collections and List to support additional data structure usage in the file.
Add allowedClasses configuration methods to support class filtering.
Add FilteredObjectInputStream to restrict object deserialization to explicitly allowed classes.
Add import statement for java.util.List to support subsequent code changes.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add imports for Collections and List utility classes to support data structure usage.
Add configuration support (command line and methods) for filtering allowed classes during deserialization.
Add class filtering to restrict object deserialization to an allowed classes list for improved security.

relevance_review.reason: Adding imports for standard Collections and List utility classes does not directly address insecure deserialization or the restriction of instantiated classes.
The group adds configuration options to filter allowed classes during deserialization, directly addressing the root cause of insecure deserialization caused by unrestricted class instantiation.

The group directly addresses the insecure deserialization vulnerability by introducing a FilteredObjectInputStream and an allowed classes list, which restricts the classes that can be instantiated during deserialization.
Adding an import statement for java.util.List is a structural/utility change and does not directly address the insecure deserialization vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This creates an unrestricted ObjectInputStream which is used to deserialize untrusted data, leading to insecure deserialization.
This line actually performs the insecure readObject() call on the unrestricted stream.
This instantiates the bridge that uses the unrestricted ObjectInputStream for TcpSocketServer.
This instantiates UdpSocketServer with the unrestricted ObjectInputStream bridge.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement creates an unrestricted ObjectInputStream which is used to deserialize untrusted data, leading to the insecure deserialization vulnerability.
This instantiates the bridge that uses the unrestricted ObjectInputStream for TcpSocketServer.
This instantiates UdpSocketServer with the unrestricted ObjectInputStream bridge.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: [LOG4J2-583] TCP and UDP socket servers should be able to handle XML log events. Refactor current code for XML and JSON.
bic_trace_step.reason: The snippet shows the creation of a standard 'ObjectInputStream' in 'wrapStream' and the unrestricted deserialization call 'inputStream.readObject()' in 'readLogEvent'. There is no object input filtering applied, which directly aligns with the root cause of insecure deserialization.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit ee87a184c7ccbfe03234782ebd6cc76c954a4a3c
Author: Gary Gregory <ggregory@apache.org>
Date:   Wed Sep 21 16:19:43 2016 -0700

    [LOG4J2-1604] Log4j2 TcpSocketServer in background.

diff --git a/log4j-core/src/main/java/org/apache/logging/log4j/core/net/server/TcpSocketServer.java b/log4j-core/src/main/java/org/apache/logging/log4j/core/net/server/TcpSocketServer.java
index 6dfce72de4..72845bf681 100644
--- a/log4j-core/src/main/java/org/apache/logging/log4j/core/net/server/TcpSocketServer
bic_trace_step.reason: The code contains the vulnerable construct in `createSerializedSocketServer` at lines 139-143 and 155-161, which instantiate `TcpSocketServer<ObjectInputStream>` using `ObjectInputStreamLogEventBridge` without any mechanism to restrict which Java classes can be instantiated. There is no usage of a filtering ObjectInputStream or similar protection against insecure deserialization, meaning the fix is not present.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: [LOG4J2-583] TCP and UDP socket servers should be able to handle XML log events. Refactor current code for XML and JSON.
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `SocketHandler` instantiates the default `ObjectInputStream` directly on line 51: `this.inputStream = new ObjectInputStream(socket.getInputStream());` without implementing any filtering or class restriction, which leads to insecure deserialization.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Stop running the server if the server socket is closed. Now, the super class implements Runnable, so subclasses do not n
bic_trace_step.reason: The UDPSocketServer class contains createSerializedSocketServer() which instantiates the server using ObjectInputStreamLogEventBridge (line 63). This bridge relies on the default ObjectInputStream for deserialization without any class filtering restrictions, leading to the insecure deserialization vulnerability. No validation or restriction mechanism is present in this commit.
